title: ⁠I am moving to a new apartment in...

messages.content: ⁠I am moving to a new apartment in Spain next month. According to Section 19 of the Electricity Terms, can I terminate my contract immediately? What specific notice period applies, and what happens if DEW21 offers to continue supplying me at my new address?

According to Section 19.2 of the Allgemeine Lieferbedingungen Strom (General Supply Conditions for Electricity), a customer may terminate their contract by providing six weeks' notice in writing, and by stating their future address or the market location identification number used to designate their future delivery address. The termination may take effect at the time of the move or at a later date.

Specifically:
- **Notice Period**: Six weeks (Section 19.2).
- **Notification Content**: The customer must provide their future address or the market location identification number.
- **Effectiveness**: Termination can occur either immediately upon moving in or at a later specified date.

If DEW21 offers to continue supplying the customer at their new place of residence under the existing contract terms, as per Section 19.3, this offer must be made within two weeks of receiving the termination notice and if supply is possible. The supply will commence upon moving in provided that the customer has informed DEW21 of the moving date in a timely manner.

Sources: 
- [Allgemeine_Lieferbedingungen_Strom.pdf_en.txt] Sections 19.2, 19.3
